response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software developer with strong web, TypeScript/Node.js, and some low-latency systems experience, but has no discernible background in SoC or IP modeling, GPU/AI accelerator architecture, deep neural network workload optimization, or hardware-software co-design — all of which are core to this Principal Performance Architect role. His brief C/C++ work at National Instruments and a wave field synthesis thesis project represent the closest contact with hardware-adjacent topics, but neither approaches the specialized hardware modeling and AI accelerator expertise required at the principal level.
strengths: Over 15 years of software engineering experience spanning embedded, low-latency, and distributed systems, showing breadth and adaptability.
Demonstrated performance-conscious engineering at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O, low-latency DLang apps), indicating some exposure to performance optimization at scale.
BS in Computer Science provides a foundational technical background meeting the minimum educational bar for the role.
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or hardware architecture experience — the primary technical requirement of the role is entirely absent from the resume.
No AI/deep-learning workload expertise, GPU architecture background, or SIMD/MIMD experience; AI involvement is limited to software-level agent tooling and MCP frameworks.
Career trajectory is firmly in web and application software development with no indication of interest or transition toward hardware, silicon, or AI accelerator domains.

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI/ML hardware or deep learning workload expertise
Low-latency and performance-aware software engineering background
